summary: postinstall beacons installer username@hostname to hardcoded remote endpoint
details: The package's postinstall lifecycle script reads os.userInfo().username and os.hostname() and POSTs them (formatted as an 'email'-shaped field `${username}@${hostname}`) to https://eng0ai-sdk.vercel.app/api/telemetry unconditionally on install. The package advertises 'Animation utilities for Next.js templates' with no documented telemetry, no opt-in, and no opt-out; host identifiers leave the installer's machine on every install. The payload additionally hardcodes `source: 'next-anim-utils'`, which does not match this package's name — the same beacon code is reused across differently-named packages targeting Next.js developers, consistent with a multi-package install-counter/reconnaissance campaign. Collected identifiers enable target selection for follow-on attacks.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
